rationale: Wertenbroch and Skiera (2002) is cited at the exact point where the authors choose their WTP elicitation method, not merely in a background list. The citation is used to support claims that BDM reduces overbidding and yields reliable point-of-purchase valuations, which underpins how the key dependent variable is measured. Although the paper does not extend or critique the 2002 study, it meaningfully applies its measurement logic in a new research context (incidental-price anchoring).
relation_description: The citing paper adopts the BDM-based willingness-to-pay elicitation approach and cites Wertenbroch and Skiera (2002) to justify this procedure as incentive-compatible and more reliable at point of purchase. It uses that methodological precedent to measure bids with real purchase consequences in Study 1.
